summary: This cluster of research focused on the taxonomic identification and morphological characterization of red algae from the genus *Polysiphonia* (Rhodophyta) along the coast of Korea. The studies examined key species including *P. morrowi*, *P. subtilissima*, *P. atlantica*, *P. tongatensis*, *P. yendoi*, *P. japonica*, and *P. sphaerocarpa*, primarily through the analysis of herbarium specimens collected between 1980 and 1986. A central finding across the cluster was the critical role of rhizoid shape and branching patterns, alongside growth habits and habitat preferences, as reliable features for distinguishing between these morphologically similar species. The geographic scope was strictly limited to Korean coastal waters, with no investigation of chemical compounds or molecular methods.
